author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end, for the eighth, a psalm of David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It has been said on the sixth Psalm, that “the eighth” may be taken as the day of judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the eighth” may also be taken “for the eternal age;” for that after the time present, which is a cycle of seven days, it shall be given to the Saints.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Save me, O Lord, for the holy hath failed;” that is, is not found: as we speak when we say, Corn fails, or, Money fails.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the truths have been minished from among the sons of men” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The truth is one, whereby holy souls are enlightened: but forasmuch as there are many souls, there may be said in them to be many truths: as in mirrors there are seen many reflections from one face.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath talked vanity each man to his neighbour” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By neighbour we must understand every man: for that there is no one with whom we should work evil; “and the love of our neighbour worketh no evil.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deceitful lips, with a heart and a heart they have spoken evil things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The repetition, “with a heart and a heart,” signifies a double heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “May the Lord destroy all deceitful lips” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He says “all,” that no one may suppose himself excepted: as the Apostle says, “Upon every soul of man that doeth evil, of the Jew first, and of the Greek.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The tongue speaking great things:” the proud tongue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who have said, We will magnify our tongue, our lips are our own, who is Lord over us?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Proud hypocrites are meant, putting confidence in their speech to deceive men, and not submitting themselves to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because of the wretchedness of the needy and the sighing of the poor, now I will arise, saith the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so the Lord Himself in the Gospel pitied His people, because they had no ruler, when they could well obey.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence too it is said in the Gospel, “The harvest is plenteous, but the labourers are few.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this must be taken as spoken in the person of God the Father, who, because of the needy and the poor, that is, who in need and poverty were lacking spiritual good things, vouchsafed to send His own Son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From thence begins His sermon on the mount to Matthew, where He says, “Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will place in salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He does not say what He would place: but, “in salvation,” must be understood as, in Christ; according to that, “For mine eyes have seen Thy salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And hence He is understood to have placed in Him what appertains to the taking away the wretchedness of the needy, and the comforting the sighing of the poor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will deal confidently in Him:” according to that in the Gospel, “For He taught them as one having authority, and not as their scribes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The words of the Lord” are “pure words” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is in the person of the Prophet himself, “The words of the Lord” are “pure words.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He says “pure,” without the alloy of pretence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many preach the truth impurely; for they sell it for the bribe of the advantages of this life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of such the Apostle says, that they declared Christ not purely.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Silver tried by the fire for the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These words of the Lord by means of tribulations approved to sinners.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Purified seven times:” by the fear of God, by godliness, by knowledge, by might, by counsel, by understanding, by wisdom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For seven steps also of beatitude there are, which the Lord goes over, according to Matthew, in the same sermon which He spake on the Mount, “Blessed” are “the poor in spirit, blessed the meek, blessed they that mourn, blessed they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness, blessed the merciful, blessed the pure in heart, blessed the peacemakers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of which seven sentences, it may be observed how all that long sermon was spoken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the eighth where it is said, “Blessed” are “they which suffer persecution for righteousness’ sake,” denotes the fire itself, whereby the silver is proved seven times.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And at the termination of this sermon it is said, “For He taught them as one having authority, and not as their scribes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which refers to that which is said in this Psalm, “I deal confidently in Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou, O Lord, shalt preserve us, and keep us from this generation to eternity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7): here as needy and poor, there as wealthy and rich.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The ungodly walk in a circle round about” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 8): that is, in the desire of things temporal, which revolves as a wheel in a repeated circle of seven days; and therefore they do not arrive at the eighth, that is, at eternity, for which this Psalm is entitled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So too it is said by Solomon, “For the wise king is the winnower of the ungodly, and he bringeth on them the wheel of the wicked.—After Thine height Thou hast multiplied the sons of men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is in temporal things too a multiplication, which turns away from the unity of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence “the corruptible body weigheth down the soul, and the earthy tabernacle presseth down the mind that museth upon many things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the righteous are multiplied “after the height of God,” when “they shall go from strength to strength.”
